text: A computer is a helpful electronic device that can store information, show pictures, and help you learn new things. Computers come in different sizes from small phones to large desks.
Hardware includes the physical parts of a computer that you can touch. The main parts are the screen for viewing, keyboard for typing, mouse for pointing, and the computer box that holds the brain.
Software is the programs that tell the computer what to do. The operating system manages everything while applications like word processors and games perform specific tasks.
To turn on a computer safely, press the power button and wait for it to start. Always shut down properly by clicking the start button and selecting shut down to protect your work.
The mouse helps you point and click on items on the screen. Use the left button for most actions. Practice moving the mouse smoothly and clicking accurately on targets.
The keyboard has letters, numbers, and special keys. Press keys to type words. The spacebar adds spaces between words. The enter key starts new lines. Practice typing your name slowly and accurately.
Files are digital documents stored on the computer. Folders help organize files like drawers. The desktop shows shortcuts to programs. Keep your files organized so you can find them easily.
Icons represent programs and files on the desktop. Double-click icons to open them. The taskbar shows running programs. The start button opens a menu with all available programs.
The internet connects computers around the world. Websites provide information, videos, and services. Use a web browser to visit websites by typing addresses or searching for topics.
Web browsers like Chrome and Edge let you visit websites. Type addresses in the address bar or search using Google. Click links to go to other pages. Use bookmarks to save favorite sites.
Search engines help find information online. Type questions or keywords in the search box. Look at results and click helpful links. Check multiple sources to verify information.
Email sends messages instantly over the internet. Create messages with subject and body. Add attachments like photos. Check your inbox regularly. Use strong passwords to protect your account.
Online safety means protecting yourself and others. Never share personal information with strangers. Use strong passwords. Tell trusted adults about uncomfortable online experiences.
Social media connects people online. Share updates, photos, and messages. Be kind and respectful. Privacy settings control who sees your posts. Don't share everything with everyone.
Word processors create and edit documents. Type text and format with bold, italic, and colors. Save work regularly. Print or share documents electronically.
Spreadsheets organize numbers in rows and columns. Use formulas to calculate totals. Create charts to show data visually. Save and share spreadsheet files.
Online shopping lets you buy items from internet stores. Compare prices and read reviews. Use secure payment methods. Check return policies before purchasing.
Online banking manages money securely. Check balances and pay bills. Use strong security. Never share banking information. Monitor accounts for suspicious activity.
Digital photos store pictures as computer files. Organize photos in folders. Edit brightness and size. Back up important photos to prevent losing them.
Watch videos and listen to music online. Use sites like YouTube for videos. Streaming services provide movies and songs. Respect copyright and age restrictions.
Keep computers healthy with regular maintenance. Update software for security. Clean temporary files. Back up important data. Restart when problems occur.
Most computer problems have simple solutions. Restart frozen programs. Update software. Clear browser cache. Search error messages online for help.
Cloud storage saves files online. Access files from any device. Automatic backup protects data. Share files securely with others.
Mobile devices run apps for various tasks. Download from official stores. Keep apps updated. Protect devices with passwords and security features.
Be a good digital citizen online. Respect others and follow rules. Protect privacy. Use technology positively. Stay informed about digital issues.
